release_notes:
Alpha 4.0.10.22 - 2026-05-12 - VIES workbook-wide dedup + canonical
tab palette + duplicate-option merge (Génesis 2026-05-12).

1. CRITICAL VIES bug — false negatives caused by per-row queries.
Files with hundreds of invoices to the same supplier were triggering
hundreds of identical VIES calls. VIES rate-limits each member-state
service, so the 30th+ call started returning MS_UNAVAILABLE and the
validator reported active numbers as `invalid`. Solution:
`apply_vies_to_workbook` now does WORKBOOK-WIDE dedup — collects
every (cc, vat) pair across Issued + Received, queries each unique
key ONCE, then propagates the cached result to every referencing
row (re-scoring the name match per-row).

2. Tab colors. Génesis: "no haya ninguna tab negra, y primen los
colores azul, amarillo y naranja, excepto Excluded y Aged en rojo".
New `_apply_marosa_tab_palette` in vatify_writer enforces:
NAVY    -> Issued, Received, SAP Report (+ unknown sheets)
YELLOW  -> Country Information
ORANGE  -> PIVOT, ESPL, Summary tab, VATify Comments,
VAT Return, Pre-filled VATR, Audit Checklist
RED     -> Excluded, Aged
Invoked from `_reorder_workbook_tabs`, which is the last step
before save — so the palette is the last thing applied.

3. Duplicate VIES options merged. Every tab had TWO VIES toggles:
"Run VIES validation" in Comments + "Run VAT validation (EU VIES +
UK HMRC)" in Extras. Génesis: "ambas opciones deben estar
agrupadas". Kept the existing Extras toggle (more descriptive
label) and added the "Also check domestic / non-EU codes"
sub-toggle directly under it. Removed the redundant Comments one.
`_<m>_vies_var` is now an alias of `_<m>_vat_val_var` so downstream
code that reads either name sees the same state.

4. VIES ETA estimation. Old code added a flat +25s per file. Real
cost is ~1.5s per UNIQUE VAT number. New helper
`_estimate_vies_seconds_for_enhance` peeks at each queued file,
counts uniques via `count_unique_vats_in_workbook`, caches the
count on the file entry, and multiplies by 1.5s.

Suite total: 345 passed, 4 skipped.
